req.title: Close encounter (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall close an Encounter; closed Encounters shall reject further modifications except for addendum Observations. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-032 defines three orthogonal concerns: (1) the happy-path state transition open→finished, (2) the rejection of modifications on a closed encounter (error pathway), and (3) boundary conditions on timestamps and numeric fields. STC drives the positive test (valid open→close transition), NEG drives the modification-after-close test (409 enforcement), and BVA drives the boundary test (timestamp precision at midnight and numeric field at max/min edge). A fourth DT test covers the addendum-Observation carve-out (the one permitted post-close action), ensuring the decision table of allowed vs. forbidden operations is fully exercised. All tests create their own encounter via a fixture to guarantee isolation and idempotency.

tests.name: test_close_open_encounter_sets_status_finished
actions: POST /Encounter to create a new encounter with status=in-progress
trace: R-HLT-032: clinician shall close an Encounter
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import create_encounter, close_encounter, get_encounter

def test_close_open_encounter_sets_status_finished(<auth_headers>, <base_url>):
    enc = create_encounter(<base_url>, <auth_headers>, status="in-progress")
    enc_id = enc["id"]

    resp = close_encounter(<base_url>, <auth_headers>, enc_id)

    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "finished"
    assert "end" in body.get("period", {}), "period.end must be set on close"

tests.name: test_modify_closed_encounter_returns_409
preconditions: An Encounter exists and has already been closed (status=finished)
actions: POST /Encounter to create encounter with status=in-progress
PUT /Encounter/{id} attempting to change a non-addendum field (e.g. reasonCode)
expected: Response body contains an error message referencing closed encounter
trace: R-HLT-032: closed Encounters shall reject further modifications
R-HLT-032: error pathway 409 when modifying closed encounter
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import create_encounter, close_encounter, update_encounter

def test_modify_closed_encounter_returns_409(<auth_headers>, <base_url>):
    enc = create_encounter(<base_url>, <auth_headers>, status="in-progress")
    enc_id = enc["id"]
    close_resp = close_encounter(<base_url>, <auth_headers>, enc_id)
    assert close_resp.status_code == 200

    patch_payload = {"resourceType": "Encounter", "id": enc_id,
                     "reasonCode": [{"text": "post-close edit attempt"}]}
    resp = update_encounter(<base_url>, <auth_headers>, enc_id, patch_payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 409, f"Expected 409, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "closed" in body.get("issue", [{}])[0].get("diagnostics", "").lower() \
        or "finished" in str(body).lower()

tests.name: test_close_encounter_boundary_midnight_timestamp_and_numeric_extremes
preconditions: An Encounter can be created with an explicit start timestamp
actions: POST /Encounter with period.start set to exact midnight UTC (00:00:00Z) and a numeric field at its documented maximum value
expected: period.end is a valid ISO-8601 timestamp after or equal to period.start
Numeric field value is preserved exactly (no truncation or overflow)
trace: R-HLT-032: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from <helper_fn> import create_encounter, close_encounter

MIDNIGHT_UTC = "2024-03-15T00:00:00Z"
MAX_NUMERIC = 2147483647  # INT32_MAX as boundary value

def test_close_encounter_boundary_midnight_timestamp_and_numeric_extremes(
        <auth_headers>, <base_url>):
    enc = create_encounter(
        <base_url>, <auth_headers>,
        status="in-progress",
        period_start=MIDNIGHT_UTC,
        length_value=MAX_NUMERIC
    )
    enc_id = enc["id"]

    resp = close_encounter(<base_url>, <auth_headers>, enc_id)

    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "finished"
    end_ts = body.get("period", {}).get("end", "")
    assert end_ts, "period.end must be populated"
    end_dt = datetime.fromisoformat(end_ts.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
    start_dt = datetime.fromisoformat(MIDNIGHT_UTC.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
    assert end_dt >= start_dt, "period.end must not precede period.start"
    assert body.get("length", {}).get("value") == MAX_NUMERIC, \
        "Numeric field must survive round-trip at INT32_MAX boundary"

tests.name: test_addendum_observation_allowed_on_closed_encounter
actions: POST /Encounter to create encounter with status=in-progress
POST /Observation with encounter reference and category=addendum
expected: Observation is linked to the closed encounter
Encounter status remains finished (not changed by addendum)
trace: R-HLT-032: closed Encounters shall reject further modifications EXCEPT for addendum Observations
R-HLT-032: decision table - addendum is permitted post-close
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import create_encounter, close_encounter, create_observation, get_encounter

def test_addendum_observation_allowed_on_closed_encounter(<auth_headers>, <base_url>):
    enc = create_encounter(<base_url>, <auth_headers>, status="in-progress")
    enc_id = enc["id"]
    close_resp = close_encounter(<base_url>, <auth_headers>, enc_id)
    assert close_resp.status_code == 200

    obs_payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"code": "addendum"}]}],
        "encounter": {"reference": f"Encounter/{enc_id}"},
        "code": {"text": "Addendum note"}
    }
    obs_resp = create_observation(<base_url>, <auth_headers>, obs_payload)

    assert obs_resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {obs_resp.status_code}"
    obs_body = obs_resp.json()
    assert f"Encounter/{enc_id}" in str(obs_body.get("encounter", ""))

    enc_check = get_encounter(<base_url>, <auth_headers>, enc_id)
    assert enc_check.json()["status"] == "finished", "Encounter must stay finished"
